evidence.text: Implement and validate lifecycle guardrails for Agentic SDLC gate dispatch

evidence.title: Implement and validate lifecycle guardrails for Agentic SDLC gate dispatch
users: repository maintainers evolving Agentic SDLC orchestration
downstream agents relying on deterministic gate dispatch and run-record semantics
desired_outcomes: Lifecycle gate dispatch applies required G1 and G2 guardrails consistently for relevant tasks.
Run records and gate state remain valid, reviewable, and traceable.
Validation and tests catch regressions in dispatch and gate-binding behavior.
in_scope: Lifecycle guardrails for Agentic SDLC gate dispatch.
Validation behavior for the targeted guardrails.
Run-record behavior needed to support the targeted guardrails.
Automated tests covering the targeted scenarios.
out_of_scope: Broader SDLC workflow redesign outside gate-dispatch guardrails.
Repository-wide overlay cleanup such as authority resolution, command confirmation, or environment metadata.
Production deployment, persistent environment mutation, or release authorization changes.
constraints: Preserve G1 and G2 behavior required by the active quick profile and dispatch plan.
Keep lifecycle quality gates separate from human mutation gates.
Maintain traceability and schema-valid run-record behavior.
Do not rely on unavailable portable knowledge sources.

assumptions: This change is repository-internal orchestration work, not user-facing product functionality.
Success can be measured by validator and test behavior without requiring deployment evidence.

measurable_success_criteria: Dispatch planning for this task continues to require G1 and G2 on July 24, 2026.
Targeted validator or status checks remain structurally valid after the guardrail change.
Automated tests cover positive and negative gate-dispatch scenarios relevant to the guardrails.
Run-record artifacts produced for the targeted workflow remain traceable and schema-valid.

evidence.reason: Refresh G1 review evidence to reflect resolved overlay authority assignments and local environment classification.
